concept: Teaching the Machine to See: to retire a 70-year hand count of Bristol Bay sockeye, a person still has to mark the fish one at a time so a drone's model can learn to watch, and until it is tested the towers keep the count
palette: sockeye crimson, glacial pale-blue river, river-gravel sage-green, cedar tower-wood, a phosphor-yellow human label mark, deep charcoal
references: The New York Times 'How AI sees' explanatory annotation graphics (clean human-in-the-loop labeling aesthetic)
Bloomberg Businessweek field-notebook data editorial (hand marks over a clean plate)
style_mode: editorial field-notebook: a teaching worksheet where a human yellow mark sits over a clean schematic river, and the machine learns to echo it

fingerprint.palette: sockeye crimson, glacial pale-blue river, river-gravel sage-green, cedar tower-wood, a phosphor-yellow human label mark, deep charcoal
fingerprint.metaphor: to retire a seventy year hand count, a person still teaches the machine to see the run one fish at a time, and until it is proven the towers keep the record

divergence_note: This is the opposite film from the last two. 07-09 volcano was an eye-level immersive instrument-readout that RISES (a spectrogram climbing out of a mountain); 07-08 caribou was a top-down naturalistic map that TRAVERSES a herd horizontally. This one is a macro-closeup editorial-schematic that DESCENDS: a phosphor-yellow human label mark dropping onto ONE sockeye in a fullbleed split, human-taught frame beside machine-seen frame. The hero is a single organic fish, not a landscape and not a swarm; the register is a clean teaching worksheet, not an instrument scope and not a painted scene; the palette is glacial pale-blue and sockeye crimson, sharing nothing with basalt-ember or autumn-tundra. Where those two centered the MACHINE'S perception, this one centers the HUMAN LABOR that has to precede it: the several thousand hand marks a person makes before the model can see at all.
honest_caveat: The drone's model cannot count yet. A person must first hand-label several thousand fish to teach it, it has NOT been tested against the Fish and Game tower counts (that check is the next phase), and hard cases like a shadow on the river may still need much more training. The towers keep the count this summer. Fish and Game runs the towers but is not a partner in this pilot.
onscreen_number_policy: Only doubly-confirmed figures appear as bare numerals: OVER 70 YEARS (article + independent, conservative vs the 1926 tower record), WORLD'S LARGEST SOCKEYE RUN (ADFG), 08:30 launch (article verbatim), SEVERAL THOUSAND LABELS (article), Wood River escapement goal 700,000 TO 2,800,000 (ADFG primary). NOT shown as bare numerals (single-sourced / unconfirmed): the year 1955, a count of nine tower sites, the 6.43M Wood River forecast.
cultural_note: Bristol Bay sockeye are the subsistence and cash backbone of the region's Yup'ik, Alutiiq and Dena'ina communities. Frame the technology as augmenting a stewarded tradition, never as outside technologists fixing the fishery. No Native iconography or unverified Native words on screen. Aerial imagery over subsistence rivers raises data-governance questions the region's tribes and CDQ organizations should be consulted on and compensated for; the tradition is being augmented, not replaced.

beats.vo: Every summer, Bristol Bay's sockeye come back by the million.
beats.shows: cold open on ONE phosphor-yellow mark descending onto a single crimson sockeye, then pull to a high aerial: a crimson mass fills a pale-blue channel, WORLD'S LARGEST SOCKEYE RUN eyebrow
beats.means: plant the thesis frame one: a person marks the fish; then the stake

beats.vo: For seventy years, people have counted them by hand,
beats.shows: a clean OVER 70 YEARS BY HAND stamp settles over the river plate
beats.means: the long human tradition

beats.vo: from wooden towers,
beats.shows: match cut: the river's bright edge becomes a cedar counting-tower post rising on the bank
beats.means: the tower, the human vantage

beats.vo: a counter watches the water and clicks,
beats.shows: macro of a hand on a mechanical tally counter, the number ticking up
beats.means: the human labor itself

beats.vo: ten minutes of every hour, around the clock.
beats.shows: a HUD ring labeled 10 MIN / HR fills, an AROUND THE CLOCK band beneath it
beats.means: the relentless cadence

beats.vo: That count sets the season, when to fish, and when to stop.
beats.shows: the tally number physically flips a fleet marker from GO to HELD over the river, a small 700,000 TO 2,800,000 eyebrow
beats.means: one see-able cause and effect: the count holds power

beats.vo: Now a drone lifts from a box at eight thirty,
beats.shows: a weatherproof box opens and a drone rises, an 08:30 clock booting beside it
beats.means: the machine arrives

beats.vo: and films the run from above.
beats.shows: the drone's-eye frame drops down onto the bright river surface
beats.means: a new vantage on the same water

beats.vo: But it cannot count. Not yet.
beats.shows: the aerial frame freezes, a bold NOT YET sits over a blank zeroed counter
beats.means: the honest limit stated up front

beats.vo: First, a person has to teach it,
beats.shows: a phosphor-yellow line and a human cursor bloom into the still frame
beats.means: the human-in-the-loop (a bloom, not a tally)

beats.vo: marking each fish that crosses a line,
beats.shows: yellow label marks drop one by one onto individual sockeye at the line
beats.means: the teaching labor, drawn

beats.vo: several thousand of them.
beats.shows: a label tally races upward toward a SEVERAL THOUSAND LABELS readout
beats.means: the scale of the human work

beats.vo: Only then can the model pick the fish out on its own.
beats.shows: double exposure: the marked fish resolve into one clean learned exemplar sockeye
beats.means: the model forms from the marks

beats.vo: (payoff) the machine echoes the marks.
beats.shows: machine-drawn boxes snap across the run in a grid, unaided
beats.means: the payoff, the machine sees on its own

beats.vo: It has not been tested against the towers yet.
beats.shows: hard cut to a split: left the model's clean count climbing, right the same river held back, a thin NOT YET TESTED eyebrow along the seam
beats.means: the caveat, shown as a held discrepancy not a stamp

beats.vo: And a shadow on the river can still fool it.
beats.shows: a dark shadow band crosses the right frame, one sockeye dims and is missed beneath it, a gap opens in that tally
beats.means: the real failure mode, drawn honestly

beats.vo: So the counters still climb the towers,
beats.shows: pull out: the cedar tower and a human silhouette return, the drone small above
beats.means: humans still hold the count

beats.vo: and the model learns to watch beside them.
beats.shows: drone and tower over one river, the human mark and the machine box glowing together
beats.means: side by side, not replaced

beats.vo: The hand still teaches.
beats.shows: the phosphor-yellow human mark re-lights warm on a single fish
beats.means: the human's role re-affirmed at the end

beats.vo: The count still comes home.
beats.shows: the count number settles and locks into place over the river, the branded outro begins to rise
beats.means: the payoff lands home

shots.centered: the crimson river of sockeye descending through the center of frame
shots.means: open on the stake: the run returns to Bristol Bay

shots.centered: a hand on a mechanical tally counter, the number ticking, dead center
shots.means: the descending river edge matches to the tower post: the long human count

shots.centered: the drone rising from its box, then its eye-view dropping onto the river
shots.means: the machine boots into the same river the counter watches

shots.centered: the yellow line dead center, a human mark dropping onto one exemplar sockeye
shots.means: the yellow line carries from the drone view into the teaching panel: the human labor

shots.centered: the learned exemplar at center, then machine boxes snapping across the run
shots.means: the human marks become the model, then the model echoes them across the run

shots.centered: the split seam at center, one sockeye slipping through a shadow on the right
shots.means: the honest limit: not tested against the towers, and a shadow still hides a fish

shots.centered: the tower, the human, and the drone over one descending river, the count settling center
shots.means: resolution: the hand still teaches, the model watches beside it, the count comes home
